title: B.Sc. (Hons) Forensic Science with specialization in Cyber and Multimedia Forensics
content: The B.Sc. (Hons) Forensic Science program with a specialization in Cyber and Multimedia Forensics is an undergraduate course designed to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of forensic science principles, focusing on the investigation of cybercrimes and the analysis of digital and multimedia evidence. This specialization prepares students for careers in digital forensics, cybersecurity, law enforcement, and related fields, equipping them with the necessary skills to handle complex cyber investigations and multimedia evidence analysis. The curriculum combines core forensic science courses with specialized training in cyber and multimedia forensics, supplemented by practical training, laboratory work, internships, and field experiences. Graduates of the B.Sc. (Hons) Forensic Science with specialization in Cyber and Multimedia Forensics have a wide range of career opportunities, including: Digital Forensic Analyst, Cybersecurity Specialist, Multimedia Forensics Expert. XII with Science with minimum 50% marks. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list For Migration from Other Universities: 1) B.Sc (Hons/Hons with Research) in Forensic Science Ist yr. with minimum 50% marks or 5.0 CGPA on a 10 point scale 2) XII with Science with minimum 50% marks. 01 Foundational & Specialized Knowledge: Provide a strong base in core forensic science while specializing in digital evidence recovery, cybercrime investigation, and multimedia forensics (image/video/audio analysis). 02 Technical & Practical Expertise:Train students in digital forensic tools (e.g., FTK, EnCase, Cellebrite), network security, malware analysis, and metadata extraction for legal investigations. 03 Legal & Ethical Compliance: Develop understanding of cyber laws (IT Act, GDPR), chain of custody for digital evidence, and ethical hacking principles to ensure court-admissible findings. 04 Research & Problem-Solving Skills: Foster analytical thinking through case studies, mock cybercrime scenarios, and research projects on emerging threats (deepfakes, cryptocurrency fraud). 05 Industry-Ready Graduates: Prepare students for careers in cyber forensics labs, law enforcement, corporate security, or IT audits via internships, certifications (CHFI, CEH), and hands-on lab training. 01 02 03 04 05 06 07 08 01 02 03 01 Disciplinary Knowledge: Apply the knowledge of forensic concepts and applications in the field of criminal and civil investigations for the purposes of justice. 02 Problem Analysing: Develop an ability to analyse the evidences, identify and define appropriate solutions and enhance overall protocols and also equip with technological ability and problem solving skills. 03 Formulation and Design: Handle the advanced techniques of science and technology to analyze and design procedures and protocols for solving variety of problems related to real life cases. XII with Science with minimum 50% marks. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list For Migration from Other Universities: 1) B.Sc (Hons/Hons with Research) in Forensic Science Ist yr. with minimum 50% marks or 5.0 CGPA on a 10 point scale 2) XII with Science with minimum 50% marks.
